flashcards.definition: A value c where f(c) = 0; corresponds to an x-intercept.
The number of times a factor (x − c) appears; affects how the graph behaves at x = c.
The graph crosses the x-axis at that zero.
The graph touches (bounces off) the x-axis at that zero without crossing.
A degree-n polynomial has exactly n zeros (real or complex, counting multiplicity).
